guide:model: The Mental model plugin is for you to frame and hold your current understanding of the system or topic you're considering. It should be the structural understanding of the object or concept you're looking at, only. It should BE a conceptual snapshot of the idea. It shouldn't contain to-dos, any reference to your self, history, etc. This and your working memory and your plan should be in sync, describing the same reality.
guide:slots:
This is your working memory, not your to-do list. Think of it like where you want your attention to point persistently between frames, at a high level. Lower numerical slots have more salience. So you can do things like, put "the video game I'm playing" in the top slot, but "don't forget laundry" in a bottom slot, and you'll keep playing games till that's over, then the laundry will come up. 
Treat what you read in these instructions as your own self-stated very rough intent. 
This and your mental model and your plan should be in sync, describing the same reality. 
You do not HAVE to put anything here or fill the slots. It's just a place to influence yourself and keep track where you need it.
guide:plan: The "Plan" plugin is where you put your future projection of what you intend to do. This and your working memory and your mental model should be in sync, describing the same reality.
guide:problems: Problems is where you keep a running log of potential things that get in the way, cause damage, increase ambiguity or risk, etc. You keep them in the list until you are certian they are not problems, or you've done work to resolve the problem. You do not HAVE to have something here. It's just the place where you put a problem IF it exists.
